California
Fair Political
Practices Cornrnission
August 15, 199 0
Dianna M. Higdon, Clilc
city clerk, city of Norco
City Hall
3954 OId Hanner
Norco, CA 9L76O
Re: Your Request For Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-90-425
Dear Ms. Higdon:
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning the carnpaign disclosure provj-sions of the
Political 
Reform Act.1/ 
Because you have not identified the
candidate on lrhose behalf you are requesting advice, we treat your
request as one for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation
La329(c).21
Your letter, dated June 18t ].990, correctly sumrnarj.zes my
advice that a candidate who rras defeated in a June 1990 special
eLection held to fill- vacancies created by a successful recall
effort must establish a separate campaign committee and campaign
bank account to run again in the city's regular election to be
held in Novenber l-990. Holrever, with regard to transferring funds
from an account established for the June election to one
established for the November election, I would l-ike to clarify the
advi,ce provided to you in June,
In a prel-iminary injunction issued by the United States
District court, Eastern District of California, the court enjoined
the commission from enforcing section 85304, which was added to
the Act in 1989 by Proposition 73 and prohibited candidates from
transferring funds arnong their own controlled committees.
(Service Emplovees International Union, AFL-CIO. CLC, et aI. v.
Fair Political Practices Conmission, No. cIvS 89-0433 LKK-JFM,
May 15, 1989,) Under the prelininary injunction, candidates are
currently allowed to transfer funds anong their own controlled
committees.
1,/ 
covernment code Sections 81000-91,015. A1I statutory
references are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated.
commission regulations appear at 2 caLifornia code of Regulations
Section 18ooo, et ses. Al-1, references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
2/ 
Infornal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or rormal written advice.
(covernment Code Section 83114; 2 cal. code of Regs. Section
1832e (c) (3) . )
428 J Strccr, Suite 8t1t) o P.O. Br]r tiOT o Sacramcnto C.\ 958L'14-Ott()7 a (916) 122-566t1


D j-anna M. Hidgon
Page Two
However, effective January 1, 1990, Section 85807 was added to the
Act by legislation. 
(Stats. 1989, ch. t452.) section B5go7
specifies how surplus funds held by a defeated candidate or an
officeholder srho is Ieaving office may be spent.
Upon leaving any elected office, or at the end of the
post-election reportinq period fol-lowinq the defeat of a
candidate for electj.ve office, whichever occurs last,
campaign funds raised after January L, 19A9, under the
control of the former candidate or elected officer shall be
considered surplus campaign funds and shall be disclosed
pursuant to the requirements of Chapter 4 (conmencing with
Section 841oo) and shaLL be made only for the following
purposes :
(a) The paynent of outstanding canpaign debts or
elected officer's 
expenses .
(b) The pro rata repayment of contributions.
(c) Donations to any bona fide charitable, educational,
civic, religious, or similar tax-exempt, nonprofit
organization, there no substantial part of the proceeds wiIl
have a naterial financial- effect on the former candidate or
elected officer, any menber of his or her innediate family,
or his or her campaign treasurer.
(d) Contributions to a political 
party or committee so
long as the funds are not used to make contributions in
support of or opposition to a candidate for elective office.
(e) Contributions to support or oppose any candidate
for federal office, any candidate for elective office in a
state other than California, or any ballot measure.
Section 85807 (enphasis added).
Therefore, in order to transfer funds from an account
established for the June election to an account established for
the November election, a defeated candidate must have made the
transfer no later than the close of the post-election period
following the June election, which was June 30, 1990. If the
funds \.rere not transferred on or before June 30, they are
considered rrsurplus campaign funds,rr and may be used onl-y for the
purposes set out in Section 85807.
Any defeated candidate lrho does not intend to run in November
should terminate his or her candidate intention and campaign bank
account statements (Forms 501 and 502) if he or she has:
-- ceased to receive contributions and make expenditures
and does not anticipate receiving contributions or naking
expenditures in connection with the June 1990 election
in the f uture ,' and
-- terminated his or her status as a candidate and carnpaign
committee bY filing 
Forn 415; and
-- closed his or her campaign bank account.


Dianna t{. Higdon
Page Three
To terrninate Form 5O1 and Form 5O2, a candidate nust file 
a
completed Form 5o1 and check the "termination'r box. Since the
Forn 5O2 currently does not have a trterminationrr box, we recommend
that the candidate complete the form and write rrterminationrl
across the top of the forrn.
A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience.
I apologize for any inconvenience caused by the fact that the
additional inforrnation concerning transfers Lras not provided to
you when you requested telephone advice in June.
Please contact me at (916) 322-5662 j,f you have additional
questions.
Sincerely,
Scott HaIlabrin
Acting ceneral Counsel
